column1: Copied from the latest tailored AI Readiness content pack.

column3: action_summary
Run an executive workshop that defines the AI thesis, top business problems, success outcomes, and sponsorship model.
Build a formal AI use case intake and scoring matrix covering value, feasibility, data, risk, ownership, and timing.
Approve a pilot portfolio with named sponsors, stage gates, funding assumptions, and decision checkpoints.
Map the top candidate workflows end to end and classify each issue as process, automation, or AI.
Document workflow pain points, transaction volume, review points, and target outcomes for each shortlisted use case.
Create pilot operating designs for the top 1–2 workflows, including trigger, user step, review rule, and KPI.
Produce a data dependency register showing the exact fields, documents, transcripts, and knowledge sources each pilot requires.
Standardise critical fields, assign dataset owners, and remediate the highest-impact data quality issues for pilot scope.
Curate the approved datasets and knowledge assets that will feed each pilot, with freshness and access rules.
Build a current-state system map showing source systems, handoffs, insertion points, and manual transfers in each pilot workflow.
Design the target integration and embedding flow for each shortlisted pilot, including support and security ownership.
Implement pilot outputs directly into the operational tool of record, such as the CRM, ticketing queue, or proposal workflow.
Standardise the workflow, remove obvious rework, and automate simple rule-based steps before enabling AI.
Clarify SOPs, exception categories, handoff rules, and reviewer triggers in the selected workflows.
Select the most mature workflow and prepare it as the first controlled AI pilot with explicit oversight design.
Publish an AI usage and data-handling standard that defines approved tools, prohibited data use, and review requirements.
Create a pilot control pack covering human approval rules, vendor-risk checks, access controls, logging, and escalation.
Embed audit review, incident escalation, and governance cadence into the live pilot operating model.
Appoint an adoption owner and build a change-readiness plan for the affected teams and managers.
Deliver role-based training for users, reviewers, managers, and support teams in each pilot workflow.
Establish manager champions, adoption feedback loops, and reinforcement actions to support scale beyond the first pilots.
Define the baseline, success criteria, and value assumptions for each priority AI use case before launch.
Build a pilot KPI scorecard with pre/post comparison rules, review cadence, and scale-or-stop thresholds.
Review AI portfolio performance on a fixed cadence and approve scale, redesign, or stop decisions based on evidence.
